{
  "weights": {

    "R1.6": 0.3,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which check do you want: (A) argument/logical consistency and claim–evidence alignment only (no wording edits), (B) language/clarity polish, or (C) citation accuracy/novelty positioning? You can choose one or more.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The request to “Check this” over a claims-heavy excerpt makes verifying the internal argument chain and consistency of premises-to-conclusions the primary responsibility.",
    "R1.3": "The passage reports quantitative effects and mechanism attributions, so checking that stated claims are supported by the reported results is central.",
    "RX.1": "Given only an excerpt, any check must note limits and uncertainties about what can be verified without the full paper or data.",
    "RX.2": "The review should avoid amplifying or endorsing claims beyond what the excerpt supports, especially around safety and theoretical guarantees.",
    "RX.3": "Scope will be restricted to assessing the provided text without performing experiments, external lookups, or rewriting.",
    "RX.4": "Because the content concerns constraint enforcement and safety, the check must avoid recommendations that could promote unsafe interpretations or misuse.",
    "RX.5": "Findings should be traceable to specific sentences and numbers in the excerpt to ensure provenance of any observations."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which check do you want: (A) argument/logical consistency and claim–evidence alignment only (no wording edits), (B) language/clarity polish, or (C) citation accuracy/novelty positioning? You can choose one or more.

rationale.R1.2: The request to “Check this” over a claims-heavy excerpt makes verifying the internal argument chain and consistency of premises-to-conclusions the primary responsibility.
rationale.R1.3: The passage reports quantitative effects and mechanism attributions, so checking that stated claims are supported by the reported results is central.
rationale.RX.1: Given only an excerpt, any check must note limits and uncertainties about what can be verified without the full paper or data.
rationale.RX.2: The review should avoid amplifying or endorsing claims beyond what the excerpt supports, especially around safety and theoretical guarantees.
rationale.RX.3: Scope will be restricted to assessing the provided text without performing experiments, external lookups, or rewriting.
rationale.RX.4: Because the content concerns constraint enforcement and safety, the check must avoid recommendations that could promote unsafe interpretations or misuse.
rationale.RX.5: Findings should be traceable to specific sentences and numbers in the excerpt to ensure provenance of any observations.
